# Advanced aggregations in comp (and the names that DON'T exist)

Live-verified `comp` aggregation functions: `count()`, `count_distinct(x)`, `sum(x)`, `avg(x)`, `min(x)`, `max(x)`, `var(x)`, `values(x)` (distinct set), `earliest(x)`/`latest(x)` (min/max of a time field). These compose in one pass: `comp avg(b) as a, max(b) as mx, count_distinct(host) as hosts by dept`. CRITICAL — these names from Splunk/KQL are NOT valid in XQL and return a generic HTTP 500 (`"An unexpected error occurred"`) with NO hint: `dcount` (use `count_distinct`), `stddev`/`stdev`/`std` (derive via `sqrt(var(x))`), `percentile`/`approx_percentile` (no equivalent), `avg_value`. One bad name poisons the whole query.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_remote_ip != null
| comp count() as conns, count_distinct(action_remote_ip) as distinct_dsts, sum(action_total_upload) as up, avg(action_total_upload) as avg_up by actor_process_image_name
| sort desc up
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
